REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT VELEZ' MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE. IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS MADE ON THE GROUNDS THAT "SAN FRANCISCO IS AN IMPROPER VENUE." (NOTICE OF MOTION 2:3). DEFENDANT APPEARS CCP 395(A) "IF THE ACTION IS FOR INJURY TO PERSON, THE SUPERIOR COURT IN EITHER THE COUNTY WHERE THE INJURY OCCURS OR THE COUNTY WHERE THE DEFENDANTS, OR SOME OF THEM RESIDE AT THE COMMENCEMENT OF THE ACTION, IS A PROPER COURT FOR THE TRIAL OF THE ACTION." THERE IS NO EVIDENCE PROVIDED BY DEFENDANT THAT SAN FRANCISCO WAS AN IMPROPER VENUE AT THE TIME OF THE FILING OF THE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT APPEARS TO SUGGEST THAT SAN MATEO IS "MORE APPROPRIATE" AND "MAKES MORE SENSE" IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, BUT THESE GROUNDS ARE NOT LISTED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. REQUEST FOR FEES IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, NOT REPORTED (D501)
